name: Tacoma/ Pierce County Habitat for Humanity
description_short: Tacoma/Pierce County Habitat for Humanity offers affordable homeownership to income-qualified residents through a restricted resale/land lease model with mortgages starting at $1,600/month.
description_long:
Tacoma/Pierce County Habitat for Humanity is a nonprofit organization dedicated to providing safe and affordable housing to families in Pierce County. Founded on the principle of partnership and self-reliance, the organization works with income-qualified residents to help them achieve stable homeownership. The organization is actively planning to sell 120 homes over the next 3-5 years and actively recruits qualified buyers through their homeownership program.

The organization offers affordable mortgage products exclusively to income-qualified Pierce County residents. Prospective homeowners can purchase homes for less than $300,000 with monthly mortgage payments as low as $1,600 (inclusive of taxes and insurance) and down payments under $3,000. The program uses a restricted resale/land lease model to maintain affordability long-term. To qualify, households must have a minimum income of $60,000 (not exceeding 80% Area Median Income), a credit score of 620 or higher, and willingness to complete homeowner preparation workshops. The first step is attendance at a free Homeowner Orientation.

Tacoma/Pierce County Habitat for Humanity distinguishes itself through its nonprofit status and community-focused mission rather than profit maximization. The organization offers volunteer opportunities for community members to participate in construction, operates Habitat Stores for employment and donations, and provides financial education through homeowner preparation workshops and foreclosure prevention workshops. The restricted resale/land lease model ensures homes remain affordable for future buyers, creating long-term community stability.

The program serves a specific population—income-qualified families earning $60,000-$100,800 annually (80% AMI for Pierce County). While the 620+ credit score requirement is reasonable, the program's limited availability (120 homes over 3-5 years) means acceptance is competitive. Prospective buyers should understand this is homeownership with ongoing support and community expectations, not a quick loan product.
pros: Mortgages as low as $1,600/month including taxes and insurance, making homeownership accessible to working families
Down payments under $3,000, significantly lower than conventional mortgage requirements
Restricted resale/land lease model ensures homes remain affordable for future generations
Free Homeowner Orientation and required homeowner preparation workshops provide financial education
Credit score requirement of 620+ is achievable for many borrowers rebuilding credit
Household income definition includes child support, disability, and SSI, broadening eligibility
Additional community support through foreclosure prevention workshops and volunteer opportunities
Planning to sell 120 homes in 3-5 years shows substantial pipeline and organizational capacity
cons: Limited availability—only 120 homes planned over 3-5 years in entire Pierce County service area
Requires minimum household income of $60,000, excluding lower-income residents despite nonprofit mission
Restricted resale/land lease model limits equity building and future home value appreciation compared to traditional mortgages
Mandatory participation in homeowner preparation workshops adds time commitment before purchase approval
Credit score minimum of 620 still excludes borrowers with severely damaged credit histories
best_for: Income-qualified Pierce County families earning $60,000-$100,800 with stable employment seeking affordable homeownership
First-time homebuyers with 620+ credit scores but limited down payment savings (under $3,000)
Families struggling with rising rent seeking long-term housing stability and wealth-building through homeownership
Community-minded individuals interested in volunteer construction work or donating to affordable housing initiatives
diagnosis: Best for working families in Pierce County earning $60,000-$100,800 who have credit scores of 620+ and can commit to the homeownership program, including required workshops and community participation. The main caveat is extremely limited availability (120 homes over 3-5 years for an entire county) and the restricted resale model that prioritizes long-term affordability over individual equity appreciation—this is mission-driven homeownership, not a conventional investment product.
services: Affordable mortgage origination with low monthly payments and down payments
Free Homeowner Orientation for prospective buyers
Homeowner preparation and financial education workshops
Foreclosure prevention workshops for at-risk homeowners
Volunteer construction opportunities for community members
Habitat Store employment and shopping for donated goods
Donation and fundraising programs to support housing initiatives
Women Build volunteer events for female-identifying construction learners
New volunteer orientation and background check processing
Income eligibility assessment and credit score evaluation
Restricted resale/land lease model property management
Post-purchase homeowner support and community partnerships
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Tacoma/Pierce County Habitat offers affordable homeownership to income-qualified residents with mortgages starting at $1,600/month.
